Mordheim
⌂ BitDeath Labs
📖 Game Rules
⚔ Campaign Guide
How to run a Mordheim campaign
⚙ New Campaign ▸
⚙ One-Time Setup
1
Custom Ruleset (optional)
In GM Mode, build a house-rules ruleset and share the exported JSON with players for consistent pricing and item availability.
2
Players Build Warbands
Each player creates and saves their warband in the Warband Builder, then sends the GM a Warband Sync Code — found in ☩ Builder → 📤 Share → Warband Sync Code. This compact code carries the full roster and is the easiest way to hand off a warband. A JSON export also works if preferred.
3
Create the Campaign
Campaign Manager → New Campaign. Add warbands from the ⊕ From Roster dropdown, or use the ⬆ Import button in the warband list to import a player’s Warband Sync Code or JSON file directly from the creation dialogue.
📋 Before Every Session
4
Import Updated Warbands
Ask each player to send a Warband Sync Code (☩ Builder → 📤 Share) after completing their AAR and any post-battle purchasing. Import via GM Mode → Warband Standings → Import — paste the sync code or upload a JSON. Required whenever a player self-manages their AAR, spends gold, or hires new recruits.
5
Send the Round’s Code to Players
Go to Campaign Manager → ⟳ Advance Round. For Round 1, copy the full 📡 Sync Code and send it to all players. From Round 2 onward, use the 📡 Update Code instead — it only contains what changed since the last round, making it far shorter to share. Players tap ⚡ Apply Sync Code for either type.
6
Validate All Rosters
Click ✓ Validate All in GM Mode → Warband Standings. Resolve any ❌ errors before play begins.
7
Assign Scenarios & Matchups
Use the Scenario Randomizer in GM Mode or pick scenarios manually and pair up the round’s matchups.
⚔ During the Battle
8
Track Combat & Mark OOA
Each player opens their warband and uses ⚔ Combat Tracker to mark warriors as ⊘ OOA as they fall. OOA status carries into the AAR automatically. If a player forgets, injuries can be set manually in AAR Step 1.
📜 After the Battle
9
Run the After Action Report
Click ⚔ After Action Report in Campaign Manager (or GM Mode) and complete all 6 steps:
1 Battle result, injuries & prisoner assignment
2 XP earned & advance rolls
3 Exploration chart & special finds
4 Sell wyrdstone & collect gold
5 Veteran XP pool
6 Rarity roll → ✓ Complete & Save
10
Resolve Prisoners
GM Mode → ⛓ Prisoners. Resolve each captive with 💰 Ransom, 🔄 Exchange, or ⛓ Sell to Slavers. Players can also action held prisoners from their own Warband Builder.
🏪 Between Sessions
11
Players Visit the Trading Post
Players spend wyrdstone proceeds, buy equipment, and hire new warriors. When done, each generates a Warband Sync Code from ☩ Builder → 📤 Share and sends it to the GM — ready for Step 4 next session.
↩ Return to Step 4 — Import Warband Sync Codes & advance the round
⚠ Campaign In Progress ▸
Use this flow when players are joining a campaign already in progress and need to build their warbands from scratch mid-campaign. All compliance checks are temporarily bypassed so existing warbands can be converted.
⚙ GM Setup
1
Enable the GM Override
In GM Mode → Homebrew Settings, enable Override: Mid-Campaign List Compliance. This bypasses campaign validation app-wide so that non-campaign lists can be imported into the campaign. Disable it again once all warbands have been converted.
2
Add the Campaign to Your App
If you don’t already have the campaign in the Campaign Manager, create it now. You’ll need it active to import warbands and generate the conversion sync code.
📋 Players Build Their Lists
3
Players Build as Non-Campaign Lists
Tell each player to open the Warband Builder and build their warband as a non-campaign list — do not enable Campaign Warband in the setup panel. This lets them freely add skills, injuries, Hired Swords, and Dramatis Personae to reflect their warband’s current state mid-campaign.
4
Players Send Their Sync Code to the GM
Once the list is complete and saved, each player goes to ☩ Builder → 📤 Share → Warband Sync Code and sends the code to the GM. The GM imports it via GM Mode → Warband Standings → Import and adds the warband to the campaign.
⚔ GM Converts the Lists
5
Generate a Sync Code and Send to Players
In Campaign Manager, generate a ⚡ Sync Code and send it to each player. When a player applies this code in ☩ Builder → 📤 Share → Apply Sync Code, their non-campaign list is automatically converted to a campaign warband attached to the campaign. The app will show a CONVERT confirmation before applying.
6
Disable the Override & Continue Normally
Once all warbands are converted, return to GM Mode → Homebrew Settings and disable the Override: Mid-Campaign List Compliance. The campaign now runs as normal — follow the steps in the New Campaign guide from Step 4 onward.
🔡 Text Size
100%
Rules reference by mordheimer.net — thanks for keeping Mordheim alive.
